text: They {shared|共有した/語った|/ʃɛərd/|We shared our stories.} how they use {computers|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtərz/|Computers help us work faster.}.
In the past, {computers|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtərz/|Modern computers are powerful.} were really {big|大きい|/bɪɡ/|This book is big.}, but today they can fit in our hand.
Let's watch.
Today, Anna answers that {question|質問|/ˈkwɛstʃən/|I have a question for you.}, how do you use computers?
She will play Max's new {game|ゲーム|/ɡeɪm/|Let's play a game.} later or in the future.
Anna is playing Max's video {game|ゲーム|/ɡeɪm/|This game is fun.}.
No, no, no.
So {computers|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtərz/|Computers solve complex problems.} are always changing and they help us in many ways.
I use my {phone|電話|/foʊn/|My phone is ringing.} to text and to call people.
Using Will, I can {guess|推測する|/ɡɛs/|I guess it will rain.} the future.
I need to {walk|歩く|/wɔːk/|Let's walk to the park.} more.
Where is Anna?
{Computers|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtərz/|Computers process information quickly.} are useful, but remember to use {technology|技術|/tɛkˈnɑːlədʒi/|Technology changes rapidly.} carefully.
Ana, our {question|質問|/ˈkwɛstʃən/|Can I ask a question?} is, how do you use computers?
Listen for {computer|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtər/|My computer is slow.} and {technology|技術|/tɛkˈnɑːlədʒi/|Technology evolves quickly.} words.
Using will, I can make a {promise|約束|/ˈprɑːmɪs/|I promise to help you.} for the future.
Max, I do not like to play video {games|ゲーム|/ɡeɪmz/|Video games can be fun.}.
I love this {game|ゲーム|/ɡeɪm/|This game is exciting.}.
And tomorrow I will {walk|歩く|/wɔːk/|We walk to school.} 10,000 steps, I promise.
Earlier, I {promised|約束した|/ˈprɑːmɪst/|I promised to be there.} to play Max's video game.
Oh no, the {internet|インターネット|/ˈɪntərnɛt/|The internet is fast.} is down, I have zero connection.
Now let's go back to Anna.
Now we will go back to Anna.
Tomorrow I will {take|取る/歩く|/teɪk/|Take a deep breath.} a walk for my 10,000 steps.
I will play all day.
I like the {music|音楽|/ˈmjuːzɪk/|I love classical music.}.
She is in my video {game|ゲーム|/ɡeɪm/|Let's play a game.}.
Later, I will play your {game|ゲーム|/ɡeɪm/|This game is interesting.}.
Do not {walk|歩く|/wɔːk/|Walk slowly.} now.
For me, I sometimes {work|働く|/wɜːrk/|I work hard every day.} online, so I need a good {internet|インターネット|/ˈɪntərnɛt/|The internet connects people.} connection.
Will she {like|好きになる/気に入る|/laɪk/|I like pizza.} it?
Anna, you are {bad|悪い/だめ|/bæd/|That was a bad idea.}
She {teaches|教える|/ˈtiːtʃɪz/|She teaches English.} us more about the word will.
She is {playing|プレイする/遊ぶ|/ˈpleɪɪŋ/|Kids are playing soccer.} Max's video game.
But my game is {fun|楽しい|/fʌn/|This game is fun.}
Tomorrow I think it will {rain|雨が降る|/reɪn/|It will rain today.}
I like {texting|メッセージを送る|/ˈtekstɪŋ/|I'm texting my friend.} my friends on my phone.
Will she {win|勝つ|/wɪn/|Will she win the game?}
Try my new video game {app|アプリ|/æp/|I use a fitness app.}
Oh, the {colors|色|/ˈkʌlərz/|The colors are bright.} are pretty.
Look.
We use Will to talk about future {actions|行動/動作|/ˈækʃənz/|Future actions are important.}
I use an {exercise|運動/エクササイズ|/ˈeksərsaɪz/|Exercise is good for health.} app to count my steps.
I will {play|プレイする/遊ぶ|/pleɪ/|Let's play a game.} your video game later.
Max, you are a {computer|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtər/|A computer helps us work.}
Please try it.
Keep {watching|見ている|/ˈwɑːtʃɪŋ/|Keep watching the movie.}!
And practice talking about computers with the words you learned on Let's Learn English with Anna.
Run, Anna.
I will do better in {school|学校|/skuːl/|I like my school.}
What will {happen|起こる|/ˈhæpən/|What will happen next?} next?
Thanks guys
I think you will {play|プレイする/遊ぶ|/pleɪ/|Let's play together.} tomorrow.
Oh hi!
How do you use {computers|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtərz/|Computers help us work.}?
Sometimes we need to talk about the {future|未来|/ˈfjuːtʃər/|The future looks bright.}
Hello, I'm Dr.
I {downloaded|ダウンロードした|/ˈdaʊnloʊdɪd/|I downloaded a new app.} it to your phone.
And they are getting {faster|速い/より速く|/ˈfæstər/|Computers are getting faster.} and {smarter|賢い/より賢く|/ˈsmɑːrtər/|AI is getting smarter.}
Tomorrow I will {walk|歩く|/wɔːk/|I will walk to school.} 10,000 steps.
Anna uses {technology|技術/テクノロジー|/tekˈnɑːlədʒi/|Technology helps us solve problems.} for many things, but not to {play|遊ぶ/演奏する|/pleɪ/|Let's play a game.} video games.
Okay.
Run!
But now let's hear from some {friends|友人/仲間|/frendz/|My friends are coming over.}
Welcome to my {treehouse|木の上の小屋|/ˈtriːhaʊs/|I built a treehouse in my backyard.}
I think you will {like|好む/好きである|/laɪk/|I like ice cream.} it
You help me with the {show|番組/ショー|/ʃoʊ/|This is a great TV show.}
Jill.
{Will|助動詞（意志/推量）|/wɪl/|I will help you tomorrow.} lets us make {promises|約束|/ˈprɒmɪsɪz/|I promise to help you.} and guesses about what is going to happen.
You walked a thousand {steps|歩/段階|/steps/|Take small steps forward.} yesterday.
I like to play video games on my {tablet|タブレット|/ˈtæblət/|I read books on my tablet.}
Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
And I love taking {selfies|自撮り写真|/ˈsɛlfi/|I take selfies with my friends.}
I will play your video game later.
They answer the {question|質問|/ˈkwestʃən/|I have a question for you.}, how do you use {computers|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtərz/|Computers are useful for work.}?
I use my {laptop|ノートパソコン|/ˈlæptɒp/|My laptop is very fast.} to work.
I promise.
